# TypeScript Stack Patterns

Idiomatic TypeScript for this project's stack: **Next.js (App Router) + React 19 + LangGraph.js + Playwright**, with Aurora/pgvector and Bedrock behind server boundaries. Concept-first ŌĆö link the live docs, don't memorize version-pinned APIs.

## Pull current docs first

The ecosystem moves fast and these libraries rev frequently. Before non-trivial work, pull the current page (the LangGraph JS docs now live under `docs.langchain.com/oss/javascript`):

- TypeScript release notes: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/release-notes/overview.html
- tsconfig reference: https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/
- Next.js Server/Client Components: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components
- Next.js caching: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/deep-dive/caching
- React 19: https://react.dev/blog/2024/12/05/react-19
- LangGraph.js (current home): https://docs.langchain.com/oss/javascript/langgraph/overview
- LangGraph interrupts / human-in-the-loop: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/interrupts (concepts shared; `interrupt`/`Command` exist in JS)
- Playwright best practices: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

## Core TypeScript discipline

- **`strict: true` is the floor.** Also turn on `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` (array/record access yields `T | undefined`) and consider `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` (distinguishes `{x?: T}` missing vs `= undefined`).
- **Module settings:** `moduleResolution: "bundler"` for Next.js/Vite, `"nodenext"` for plain Node libraries. Set `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` and use `import type {...}` for type-only imports ŌĆö this prevents accidental runtime imports and is required so bundlers can erase types cleanly. `isolatedModules: true` if any tool transpiles file-by-file.
- **`satisfies` over annotation** when you want a value validated against a type *without* widening its inferred literal type (e.g. config objects, route maps).
- **Prefer discriminated unions over `enum`.** Enums have surprising runtime/emit semantics under `isolatedModules`/`verbatimModuleSyntax`; `as const` objects + union types are erasable and structurally simpler.
- **`unknown`, never `any`, at trust boundaries.** Parse external data (DB rows, LLM JSON, request bodies) into `unknown`, then narrow ŌĆö ideally with a schema validator (Zod) so the type and the runtime check stay in sync. An LLM "returning JSON" is untyped input; validate it.
- **`as` casts hide bugs** ŌĆö every cast is a place the compiler stops helping. Prefer narrowing (`typeof`, `in`, custom type guards) and `satisfies`.
- **`using` / `await using`** (explicit resource management) for things needing deterministic cleanup (DB connections, browser contexts) when your target/runtime supports it.

## Next.js App Router (TS)

- **Server Components by default.** `"use client"` marks a *boundary*: every module it imports and component it directly renders joins the client bundle. Keep it as deep/narrow as possible; pass Server Components into Client Components via `children`/props rather than importing them.
- **Async request APIs.** `params`, `searchParams`, and `cookies()`/`headers()`/`draftMode()` are **Promises** in current Next.js ŌĆö type them as `Promise<...>` and `await` them. Forgetting the await is a common type/runtime error.
- **Secrets never cross the boundary.** Only `NEXT_PUBLIC_`-prefixed env vars reach the client; everything else is stripped to `""`. Put DB/Bedrock/ElevenLabs keys in server-only modules and add `import 'server-only'` at the top so a stray client import fails at build time. (`client-only` is the inverse guard.)
- **Caching is opt-in now.** `fetch` is **not** cached by default in current versions; use `{ cache: 'force-cache' }` / `next: { revalidate, tags }`, `unstable_cache` for non-fetch (DB) calls, and React `cache()` to dedupe within a render. Invalidate with `revalidateTag`/`revalidatePath` from Server Actions. v16 introduces the Cache Components model (`cacheComponents` flag) ŌĆö check which model the repo uses.
- **Server Actions (`"use server"`)** are public POST endpoints ŌĆö validate every argument server-side; never trust client-supplied IDs/authz. Args and return values must be serializable.

## React 19 (TS)

- **`ref` is a regular prop** ŌĆö `forwardRef` is no longer needed for function components; type `ref` in your props. Ref callbacks may return a cleanup function, so don't write implicit-return arrow refs (`ref={el => (x = el)}` now type-errors ŌĆö use a block body).
- **Actions & form state:** `useActionState(action, initial)` ŌåÆ `[state, dispatch, isPending]`; `useFormStatus()` (from `react-dom`) for nested submit buttons; `useOptimistic` for optimistic UI. Prefer these over hand-rolled loading/error state.
- **`use(promise)`** unwraps a promise during render (with Suspense) ŌĆö but don't create the promise *in* render; create it server-side or in an event and pass it down.
- **Effect hygiene:** don't derive state in `useEffect` (compute during render); list every dependency; remember Effects run after paint. Most "sync" logic doesn't need an Effect.

## LangGraph.js (the agent runtime)

Import from `@langchain/langgraph`. This project runs four small graphs with human approval gates.

- **Typed state via `Annotation`.** Define channels with `Annotation.Root({ field: Annotation<T>({ reducer, default }) })`; `MessagesAnnotation` for chat. The **reducer** decides how a node's partial return merges into state (append vs replace) ŌĆö getting it wrong silently drops or duplicates data. Nodes return a *partial* state object, not the whole state.
- **Build with `StateGraph`:** `.addNode`, `.addEdge`, `.addConditionalEdges(node, router)`, `START`/`END`, then `.compile({ checkpointer })`.
- **Human-in-the-loop = `interrupt()` + `Command`.** Call `interrupt(payload)` inside a node to pause and surface the payload (the JSON gate payload to the dashboard). Resume by re-invoking with `new Command({ resume: value })` ŌĆö that value becomes the return of `interrupt()`. This **requires a checkpointer** and a stable `thread_id` in `{ configurable: { thread_id } }`; reusing the id resumes the same checkpoint, a new id starts fresh.
- **Persistence:** `MemorySaver` for dev; `PostgresSaver` (`@langchain/langgraph-checkpoint-postgres`) for Aurora-backed durable threads.
- **Streaming modes** (`"values"`, `"updates"`, `"messages"`) change the shape of what you iterate ŌĆö pick deliberately.

## Playwright (demo capture + tests)

- **Locator-first, user-facing:** `getByRole`, `getByText`, `getByLabel` ŌĆö they auto-wait and auto-retry. Avoid CSS/XPath tied to implementation.
- **Web-first assertions** (`await expect(locator).toBeVisible()`) retry until true. **Never `waitForTimeout`/sleep** ŌĆö that's the #1 source of flakiness.
- **`await` every action and assertion.** A missing `await` races and flakes.
- **Strict mode:** a locator that matches >1 element throws ŌĆö narrow it.
- **Test isolation:** fresh context per test; set up auth in `beforeEach`/fixtures, don't chain tests. For deterministic demo capture, use video/tracing config and mock external network with the routing API so recordings are reproducible.

## Checklist

- [ ] `strict` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` on; module resolution matches the runtime (`bundler` vs `nodenext`).
- [ ] External/LLM/DB data enters as `unknown` and is schema-validated before use ŌĆö no `any`, minimal `as`.
- [ ] `"use client"` is narrow; no secret-bearing module is reachable from the client (guarded with `server-only`).
- [ ] `params`/`searchParams`/`cookies()`/`headers()` are awaited.
- [ ] Server Actions validate inputs and authz server-side.
- [ ] Caching is explicit (you decided cached vs dynamic), not assumed.
- [ ] LangGraph nodes return partial state with correct reducers; interrupts have a checkpointer + stable `thread_id`.
- [ ] Playwright uses role/text locators + web-first assertions; zero `waitForTimeout`; every action awaited.
- [ ] `import type` for type-only imports; discriminated unions over enums.

## Gotchas

- **`interrupt()` re-runs the node from the top on resume.** Everything in the node *before* the `interrupt` call executes again when you resume with `Command`. Put side effects (DB writes, Bedrock calls, S3 uploads) *after* the interrupt, or make them idempotent ŌĆö otherwise a gate approval double-writes evidence/artifacts.
- **`interrupt()` silently no-ops without a checkpointer.** No persistence ŌåÆ no pause point ŌåÆ the graph runs straight through the approval gate. Compile with a checkpointer and pass `thread_id`.
- **Wrong LangGraph reducer = lost or duplicated state.** Default channel behavior is overwrite; if a node should append (messages, evidence list) you must supply an appending reducer or each node clobbers the prior value.
- **`fetch` is no longer cached by default** in current Next.js ŌĆö code that assumed the old auto-cache behavior will hammer Aurora/Bedrock on every request. Be explicit.
- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_` leak:** any secret you reference without the prefix becomes `""` on the client (silent failure), and any secret you *do* prefix is shipped to every browser. Use `server-only`, not naming luck.
- **Server Actions are unauthenticated endpoints by default** ŌĆö they're callable by anyone who can reach the app. Treat every arg as hostile; check session + authorization inside the action.
- **`enum` + `isolatedModules`/`verbatimModuleSyntax`** can break or bloat emit (const enums especially). Use `as const` unions.
- **`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` surprises:** `arr[i]` and `record[key]` become `T | undefined` ŌĆö good, but you must handle the `undefined`; don't paper over it with `!`.
- **Playwright `waitForTimeout` "fixes" that aren't:** a sleep that passes locally fails on slower CI. Use auto-retrying assertions; if you truly need to wait for state, wait on a locator/response, not a clock.
- **React 19 ref-callback implicit return** now type-errors (cleanup-function inference) ŌĆö convert `ref={el => (x = el)}` to a block body.
- **`Command`/`interrupt` resume needs the *same* `thread_id`** the gate paused on. The dashboard must round-trip the exact thread id; a new id starts an empty run and the approval is lost.
- **LLM JSON typed as a hand-written `interface` is a lie** ŌĆö the model can return anything. Validate at runtime (Zod) and derive the type from the schema, so drift can't pass the compiler unnoticed.
